meta.setting: Mine store street in French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in hired force and labor pressure

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining communities, road pressure, and resource conflict.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California mining commerce and town-street material.

image_prompt.subject: two company shotgun men guarding a mine payroll wagon outside a store while miners watch from shadowed porches
image_prompt.scene: bright dusty noon; shotguns and rifles held low, locked cash box under canvas, unreadable receipts on a crate, heat shimmer, and no one standing comfortably in the street
image_prompt.composition: square tense street tableau with guards central but not heroic; watchers layered around them, payroll wagon as the real center of gravity
image_prompt.camera: waist-height duel-distance view, game cutscene tension without action pose
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Keep their guns low; the threat should live in who gets to stand in the street.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine street tension scene
render_traits: slightly simplified guard, miner, horse, and wagon silhouettes
modest texture resolution on gunmetal, dust, canvas, wood, and cloth
hard ambient occlusion under wagon, porch rails, guards, and cash box
baked harsh noon light with deep porch shadow
believable 1899 firearms, clothing, wagon, and store materials
low-threat composition rather than active gunfight
style_modifiers.palette: dust ochre, shotgun iron, wagon red-brown, canvas tan, porch black, faded denim, brass glints, and hot sky blue.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable store signs, receipts, crate labels, badges, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The guards, payroll wagon, cash box, miners, and store street are fully readable.
Every visible prop and garment belongs plausibly to 1899 labor-conflict town life.
The image shows private force and social pressure without becoming a gunfight poster.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
